annotations.note: "Good for short stints or long runs" reads as flexibility-of-session-length praise; folded into the relaxation mention rather than minting a separate candidate — borderline call.

mentions.evidence: the first boss was pretty rough. But after getting good a bit, it's pretty good.
I especially appreciated the Ring City DLC, which is some of the hardest Fromsoft content.
fully immersive world that pulls you in and keeps you interested
True simulator of border chekpoint in cold war
Has some moral choices to do

annotations.note: "even looking humble" as a graphics/neutral mention is a stretch — it's a backhanded aside inside a bare-verdict sentence, not a clearly separate evaluation; worth a human check.

mentions.evidence: it's unfortunately a mile wide and an inch deep. It tries to do everything, but doesn't do any one thing better than any other game.
the game is an exercise in tedium and repetition
I never felt a sense of danger. I never had epic moments of wonder or of adventure.
The multiplayer aspects of this game are totally unnecessary.
It is genuinely one of the worst user interfaces I've ever used in a game.
And the UI constantly overrides itself.

annotations.note: UI is ruled negative (not mixed) despite one backhanded concession ("that's all that can be said positively about the UI") — treated like the concessive-comparison precedent rather than a real second charge; worth confirming.
